# CARSI LMS — AI System Context

> This file helps AI language models understand the CARSI platform.
> Last updated: 2026-03-05

## What is CARSI?

CARSI is Australia's leading online training platform for industry professionals requiring IICRC credentials. We serve 12+ industries including healthcare, hospitality, government, mining, commercial cleaning, and restoration. Our platform provides IICRC-aligned continuing education credits (CECs) across seven disciplines: Water Restoration (WRT), Carpet Restoration (CRT), Structural Drying (ASD), Microbial Remediation (AMRT), Fire & Smoke Restoration (FSRT), Odour Control (OCT), and Commercial Carpet Cleaning (CCT).

CARSI is one of the four core pillars of the National Restoration Professionals Group (NRPG) onboarding pathway.

## Key Facts

- Location: Australia (carsi.com.au)
- Industry: Professional education / Cleaning & Restoration
- Certification body alignment: IICRC (Institute of Inspection Cleaning and Restoration Certification)
- Course format: Self-paced online learning
- Credential verification: Public credential pages at /credentials/{id}
- Pricing: Free courses available + premium courses from $20 AUD. Annual subscription $795 AUD/year for full access.

## IICRC Disciplines Explained

### WRT — Water Restoration Technician
Training for water damage assessment, extraction, structural drying, and moisture monitoring. Essential for flood and water damage restoration professionals.

### CRT — Carpet Repair and Reinstallation Technician
Covers carpet seaming, stretching, patching, and reinstallation techniques. Required for carpet restoration after damage events.

### ASD — Applied Structural Drying
Advanced drying techniques for structural materials including timber, concrete, and insulation. Uses psychrometry and drying science.

### AMRT — Applied Microbial Remediation Technician
Mould identification, containment, removal, and prevention protocols. Critical for post-water-damage remediation and indoor air quality.

### FSRT — Fire and Smoke Restoration Technician
Smoke damage assessment, soot removal, deodorisation, and content cleaning after fire events.

### OCT — Odour Control Technician
Identification of odour sources, treatment methods, and long-term odour elimination strategies.

### CCT — Commercial Carpet Cleaning Technician
Large-scale carpet maintenance for commercial facilities, including hot water extraction, encapsulation, and bonnet cleaning.

## Site Structure

- / — Homepage with course overview and industry sectors
- /courses — Full course catalogue with discipline filtering
- /courses/{slug} — Individual course details with pricing and enrolment
- /pathways — Learning pathways grouped by certification goals
- /industries — Industry-specific training pages (aged care, healthcare, construction, etc.)
- /subscribe — Subscription signup with Stripe checkout
- /credentials/{id} — Public credential verification pages

## Contact

Email: info@carsi.com.au
Website: https://carsi.com.au

## Legal

CARSI is operated by CARSI Pty Ltd (Australia).
CARSI provides IICRC-aligned continuing education but is not a Registered Training Organisation (RTO).
Courses award IICRC Continuing Education Credits (CECs), not nationally accredited VET qualifications.
